title: Courage lands in the body first
subtitle: A body-based practice for when courage is the next required step and it doesn't feel available yet.
excerpt: A short embodiment practice from Day 44 of the MONEY program. The practice gives courage a physical location before you act — hand on chest, one sentence said out loud.
sourceNotes: Inspired by Day 44 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). Embodiment structure draws on somatic anchoring practices in the public domain.

practice.whenToUse: Use immediately before taking a bold action — a call, a price set, a send button. Do it standing up.

content.text: A short embodiment practice from Day 44 of the MONEY program. The idea is simple: courage has a physical address before it has words. This practice finds it there first.
Stand up, if you can. Place your right hand flat on your chest. Press gently — not dramaticaly, just enough to feel the contact. Take one slow breath in through the nose and out through the mouth.
Now say this, once, out loud:
I trust myself to take the next right action. I am ready.
Hold the hand on chest for another breath. Then lower it and take the action.
Inspired by Day 44 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). Embodiment structure draws on somatic anchoring practices widely used in body-based therapy and performance coaching.
